reason: Critical structural inconsistencies between tasks.md and individual task JSON files. AC-2 and AC-4 have no corresponding task JSON. Critical-path task T-0063 (pipeline insertion) is missing as a JSON artifact; T-0063.json is mis-titled as a whitelist task (a duplicate of T-0062's scope) instead of the pipeline insertion described in tasks.md. T-0067.json is mis-titled as a regression gate (duplicate of T-0068 scope) instead of the persistence test described in tasks.md. A self-referencing dependency on T-0067.json was found. Execution must not proceed until these are reconciled.

gaps: No persistence round-trip test JSON exists — tasks.md describes T-0067 as persistence test, but T-0067.json is a regression gate
T-0063.json is NOT the pipeline insertion task — it describes field whitelist (src/CategoryMappingService.js), not pipeline insert (src/App.js). No JSON task covers #resolveCategory() modification.
CRITICAL — AC-2 cannot be satisfied without pipeline insertion

tasks_json: T-0062 JSON mentions AC-3 in acceptance criteria refs but only for service layer — does not address pipeline fall-through
gaps: Fall-through to AI-hint on assigned=true but unknown category requires pipeline insertion task (missing)

tasks_tasks_md: T-0063 tasks.md pipeline insertion at AI-hint slot
gaps: No JSON task inserts direct-assign check at AI-hint slot in #resolveCategory(). AC-4 has no implementation vehicle.
No explicit task ensures backward-compat test; relied on via regression gate T-0068 / T-0067 duplicate

tasks_json: T-0067 JSON (regression gate — duplicate of T-0068 per title)
gaps: T-0067 and T-0068 overlap as regression gate in JSON. tasks.md describes T-0067 as persistence test — JSON contradicts. Test count expectation (21/21) does not account for persistence test.

issues: T-0063 JSON declares no dependencies (but tasks.md says T-0062). However since T-0063 JSON describes whitelist (not pipeline), the empty deps is internally consistent — but inconsistent with tasks.md which places pipeline insertion after service method

T-0066 JSON depends on T-0063 — this only makes sense if T-0063 is the pipeline insertion task (per tasks.md). Since T-0063 JSON is actually the whitelist task, T-0066's dependency on it is semantically incorrect (test cases depend on pipeline behavior, not whitelist)
T-0067 JSON has a self-referencing dependency: [T-0067]. This is a structural bug.
T-0067 should depend on T-0062 if it's the persistence test (per tasks.md)
T-0068 should depend on T-0066, T-0067 (all test work) — currently only declares T-0067
task_dependency_analysis.note: Critical path T-0062 → T-0063 → T-0066 → T-0068 cannot be reconstructed from JSON files because T-0063 JSON scope is incorrect (whitelist instead of pipeline insert).

comments: T-0062 bundles TWO distinct concerns: (a) new getDirectAssignment() method, AND (b) #MAPPING_FIELDS whitelist + #stripFields refactor of addMapping/updateMapping. Architecture §3.1 describes these as separate subsections. Acceptable for a single low-complexity task since they're same-file changes.
T-0063 JSON is a DUPLICATE of T-0062's whitelist concern (not a separate pipeline task as intended). Should be retitled/rewritten to be the pipeline insertion task.
T-0067 JSON duplicates T-0068's regression-gate concern. Should be a persistence round-trip test task (new file tests/categoryMappingService.test.js).
All seven tasks individually estimated at low or medium complexity are within the <1 day atomic threshold.
Sprint capacity check: 7/12 tasks, under threshold.

missing_tasks.title: Insert direct-assign check into App.js #resolveCategory() pipeline
missing_tasks.rationale: Per tasks.md this task should exist but the JSON file is titled as a duplicate of T-0062 (whitelist). The JSON file must be rewritten to match tasks.md description: pipeline insertion at line 1214, DEC-0022, fall-through rule.

missing_tasks.title: Add persistence round-trip test for directAssign field
missing_tasks.rationale: Per tasks.md this task should be the persistence test (new file tests/categoryMappingService.test.js). The JSON file currently duplicates T-0068 regression gate scope.

missing_tasks.title: Update CODEBASE_ANALYSIS.md and runbook with direct-assign documentation
missing_tasks.rationale: Neither tasks.md nor any JSON task mentions updating CODEBASE_ANALYSIS.md (US-0002 alignment) or the runbook (pipeline step order, hitl semantics for direct-assign). Not strictly required for AC satisfaction but surfaced in discovery as a nice-to-have. Defer to PO or handle as follow-up.

test_coverage_plan.note: The persistence test (T-0067 per tasks.md) is not counted in the 21-test expectation. If included, total would be 22. This is a minor bookkeeping inconsistency; recommend counting persistence test as part of the sprint's test output but separately from the case-5..7 set.

risk_flags.issue: No pipeline insertion task JSON exists. AC-2 (direct-assign → assign directly) and AC-4 (insertion at AI-hint slot) have no implementation task in the JSON file set. Executing the sprint as-is would deliver the service method + UI + test stubs but no pipeline integration — the core feature would be absent.
risk_flags.resolution: Rewrite T-0063.json to be the pipeline insertion task per tasks.md description. Files: src/App.js, dependencies: [T-0062], decision refs: [DEC-0022], acceptance: [AC-2, AC-4].

risk_flags.issue: T-0067.json is mis-titled as regression gate (duplicate of T-0068) instead of persistence test. The persistence test scope described in tasks.md has no JSON representation.
risk_flags.resolution: Rewrite T-0067.json to be the persistence test (new file tests/categoryMappingService.test.js, depends on T-0062, verifies directAssign survives save/load cycle per DEC-0021).

risk_flags.issue: T-0067.json has a self-referencing dependency (depends on itself). DAG is not acyclic if taken literally.
risk_flags.resolution: Fix dependency when rewriting T-0067 as persistence test.

risk_flags.issue: T-0063.json (whitelist duplicate) and T-0062.json (whitelist+service) overlap in scope. Both target src/CategoryMappingService.js with the same #MAPPING_FIELDS change.
risk_flags.resolution: Retitle T-0063.json as pipeline insert per resolution above. The whitelist work stays in T-0062.

risk_flags.issue: Test count expectation (21 tests) does not include persistence test described in tasks.md.
risk_flags.resolution: Clarify whether persistence test makes total 22 tests, or if it's expected to be bundled with T-0067's existing count. Recommend stating 22 tests as the final regression target.

risk_flags.issue: tasks.md implementation order step 2 lists T-0063, T-0064, T-0065, T-0067 as parallel — but T-0067 (persistence test) and T-0068 (regression gate) have a serial relationship through the JSON dependency, which contradicts the parallel claim.
risk_flags.resolution: Update tasks.md to clarify: parallel phase 2 = T-0063/T-0064/T-0065/T-0067 (assuming T-0067 is persistence test). Serial: T-0067 → T-0068.

recommendations: BLOCK execution until T-0063.json and T-0067.json are rewritten to match tasks.md.
Reconcile test count (21 vs 22 with persistence test) in sprint.json success_criteria.
Optional: add a minor documentation task for CODEBASE_ANALYSIS / runbook pipeline step update (low-effort, no new acceptance criteria).
Re-run plan-verify after fixes before proceeding to /execute.
